title: Implement Trie (Prefix Tree)
description: A trie (pronounced as 'try') or prefix tree is a tree data structure used to efficiently store and retrieve keys in a dataset of strings. Implement the Trie class with: Trie() initializes the trie object; void insert(String word) inserts word into the trie; boolean search(String word) returns true if word is in the trie (full word match); boolean startsWith(String prefix) returns true if there is any word in the trie that starts with the given prefix.
constraints: word and prefix consist only of lowercase English letters
At most 3 * 10^4 calls in total will be made to insert, search, and startsWith
thinking_questions: A Trie stores words character by character. What does each node represent?
How do you mark the end of a word vs a prefix?
At each node you need up to 26 children — how do you store them?

hints.L1: Each node represents a character position. Store children (up to 26) and a flag marking if this node is a word end.
hints.L2: TrieNode with children dict (or array[26]) and is_end flag. Insert: walk/create nodes per char, set is_end at last. Search: walk nodes, check is_end. StartsWith: walk nodes, return True if all chars found.

follow_up_questions: When would you use an array[26] for children vs a HashMap? What are the tradeoffs?
How would you add a delete operation to the Trie?
How does a Trie compare to a HashSet for prefix queries?
solution_notes: TrieNode: children dict + is_end. Insert/search/startsWith all O(n). Key: is_end distinguishes 'app' from 'apple'.
